drugs.mechanismClass: partial mu-opioid receptor agonist; kappa antagonist
fibric acid derivative (PPAR-alpha agonist); OATP1B1 and CYP2C8 inhibitor
monoamine oxidase B inhibitor (nonselective at higher/transdermal doses)
NMDA antagonist antitussive; serotonin reuptake inhibitor

classRulePairs.mechanism: Gemfibrozil inhibits OATP1B1-mediated hepatic uptake and glucuronidation of statins, raising statin plasma concentrations and the risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Fibrate-statin myopathy class rule; gemfibrozil markedly increases statin exposure via OATP1B1/glucuronidation inhibition. Combination should be avoided per FDA statin labeling and ACC/AHA guidance.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Gemfibrozil inhibits OATP1B1 uptake and glucuronidation of simvastatin acid, producing large increases in statin exposure and a high risk of severe myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: FDA simvastatin labeling explicitly contraindicates concomitant gemfibrozil due to documented rhabdomyolysis risk.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Gemfibrozil inhibits OATP1B1 uptake and glucuronidation of lovastatin, sharply increasing statin exposure and the risk of severe myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: FDA lovastatin labeling explicitly contraindicates concomitant gemfibrozil due to documented rhabdomyolysis risk.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Gemfibrozil inhibits OATP1B1-mediated hepatic uptake of pravastatin, increasing statin plasma concentrations and the risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Fibrate-statin myopathy class rule; gemfibrozil is the highest-risk fibrate and should be avoided with statins per FDA statin labeling and ACC/AHA guidance.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Gemfibrozil inhibits OATP1B1-mediated hepatic uptake of rosuvastatin, increasing statin exposure and the risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: FDA rosuvastatin labeling directs avoidance of gemfibrozil and a dose cap if unavoidable, reflecting the fibrate-statin myopathy class rule.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Gemfibrozil inhibits OATP1B1-mediated hepatic uptake of pitavastatin, increasing statin exposure and the risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: FDA pitavastatin labeling contraindicates/avoids concomitant gemfibrozil, reflecting the fibrate-statin myopathy class rule.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Fenofibrate combined with a statin has additive potential to cause myopathy, though fenofibrate does not inhibit statin glucuronidation/transport and carries substantially lower risk than gemfibrozil.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Fibrate-statin myopathy class rule; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate for statin combination and may be used with monitoring per FDA labeling and ACC/AHA guidance.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Fenofibrate combined with a statin has additive potential to cause myopathy; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate and carries substantially lower risk than gemfibrozil.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Fibrate-statin myopathy class rule; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate for statin combination and may be used with monitoring per FDA labeling and ACC/AHA guidance.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Fenofibrate combined with a statin has additive potential to cause myopathy; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate and carries substantially lower risk than gemfibrozil.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Fibrate-statin myopathy class rule; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate for statin combination and may be used with monitoring per FDA labeling and ACC/AHA guidance.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Fenofibrate combined with a statin has additive potential to cause myopathy; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate and carries substantially lower risk than gemfibrozil.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Fibrate-statin myopathy class rule; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate for statin combination and may be used with monitoring per FDA labeling and ACC/AHA guidance.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Fenofibrate combined with a statin has additive potential to cause myopathy; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate and carries substantially lower risk than gemfibrozil.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Fibrate-statin myopathy class rule; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate for statin combination and may be used with monitoring per FDA labeling and ACC/AHA guidance.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Fenofibrate combined with a statin has additive potential to cause myopathy; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate and carries substantially lower risk than gemfibrozil.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Fibrate-statin myopathy class rule; fenofibrate is the preferred fibrate for statin combination and may be used with monitoring per FDA labeling and ACC/AHA guidance.

classRulePairs.mechanism: PDE5 inhibitors potentiate the cGMP-mediated vasodilation of organic nitrates, causing profound, potentially fatal hypotension.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Nitrate plus PDE5-inhibitor contraindication; co-administration is contraindicated in all PDE5-inhibitor and nitrate labeling due to risk of life-threatening hypotension.

classRulePairs.mechanism: PDE5 inhibitors potentiate the cGMP-mediated vasodilation of organic nitrates; tadalafil's long half-life prolongs the window of profound, potentially fatal hypotension.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: Nitrate plus PDE5-inhibitor contraindication; co-administration is contraindicated in all PDE5-inhibitor and nitrate labeling due to risk of life-threatening hypotension.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining an MAO inhibitor with a serotonin reuptake inhibitor causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; concomitant use and a 5-week fluoxetine washout are required per selegiline (Emsam) and fluoxetine labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining an MAO inhibitor with an SSRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SSRI co-administration with selegiline is contraindicated per selegiline (Emsam) and SSRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining an MAO inhibitor with an SSRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SSRI co-administration with selegiline is contraindicated per selegiline (Emsam) and SSRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining an MAO inhibitor with an SSRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SSRI co-administration with selegiline is contraindicated per selegiline (Emsam) and SSRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining an MAO inhibitor with an SNRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SNRI co-administration with selegiline is contraindicated per selegiline (Emsam) and SNRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining an MAO inhibitor with an SNRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SNRI co-administration with selegiline is contraindicated per selegiline (Emsam) and SNRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Tramadol inhibits serotonin reuptake; combined with an MAO inhibitor it can precipitate serotonin syndrome and also lowers the seizure threshold.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; tramadol is contraindicated with MAO inhibitors per tramadol and selegiline labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Dextromethorphan inhibits serotonin reuptake; combined with an MAO inhibitor it can precipitate serotonin syndrome and a psychotic/behavioral reaction.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; dextromethorphan is contraindicated with MAO inhibitors per selegiline (Emsam) and OTC dextromethorphan labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining a nonselective MAO inhibitor with an SSRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; concomitant use and a 5-week fluoxetine washout are required per phenelzine and fluoxetine labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining a nonselective MAO inhibitor with an SSRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SSRI co-administration with phenelzine is contraindicated per phenelzine and SSRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining a nonselective MAO inhibitor with an SSRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SSRI co-administration with phenelzine is contraindicated per phenelzine and SSRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining a nonselective MAO inhibitor with an SSRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SSRI co-administration with phenelzine is contraindicated per phenelzine and SSRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining a nonselective MAO inhibitor with an SNRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SNRI co-administration with phenelzine is contraindicated per phenelzine and SNRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Combining a nonselective MAO inhibitor with an SNRI causes excessive synaptic serotonin, precipitating serotonin syndrome.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; SNRI co-administration with phenelzine is contraindicated per phenelzine and SNRI labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Tramadol inhibits serotonin reuptake; combined with a nonselective MAO inhibitor it can precipitate serotonin syndrome and also lowers the seizure threshold.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; tramadol is contraindicated with MAO inhibitors per tramadol and phenelzine labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: Dextromethorphan inhibits serotonin reuptake; combined with a nonselective MAO inhibitor it can precipitate serotonin syndrome and a psychotic/behavioral reaction.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus serotonergic agent serotonin-syndrome class rule; dextromethorphan is contraindicated with MAO inhibitors per phenelzine and OTC dextromethorphan labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: MAO inhibitors potentiate the pressor effects of indirect-acting sympathomimetics such as pseudoephedrine, which can precipitate a hypertensive crisis.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus indirect sympathomimetic hypertensive-crisis class rule; sympathomimetic decongestants are contraindicated with MAO inhibitors per phenelzine labeling.

classRulePairs.mechanism: MAO inhibitors potentiate the pressor effects of indirect-acting sympathomimetics such as pseudoephedrine, which can precipitate a hypertensive crisis.
classRulePairs.classRuleBasis: MAOI plus indirect sympathomimetic hypertensive-crisis class rule; sympathomimetic decongestants are contraindicated with selegiline (Emsam) per its labeling.

openfdaConfirmedPairs.mechanism: Gemfibrozil strongly inhibits CYP2C8-mediated metabolism of repaglinide, markedly increasing repaglinide plasma concentrations and the risk of severe, prolonged hypoglycemia. The gemfibrozil label states this combination is contraindicated.
